summary: Iteration 12 (RBAC authz with 11 roles × 115 permissions × 11 grant groups + policy overlays + admin pages) — FULL verification. 15/15 iteration_12 backend tests pass against live ingress (https). Regression suites iter7 (27/27) and iter8+iter11 (32/32) also pass. Frontend: all 14 sidebar items for admin render, /permissions matrix is 115 rows × 11 role cols (12 incl. permission column), filter + 'Privileged only' checkbox both work (27 privileged rows). /roles, /access-review, /elevation pages load cleanly. Doctor can request elevation via 10-char-min reason dialog; admin sees Review button for pending requests. Sidebar correctly hides privileged items for patient role (0/6 leaked). Zero CSP violations observed across the 4 new pages.

minor.issue: Running iter7/iter11 (which hit /api/auth/login in fixtures) immediately after any 40-burst bad-login suite still trips the 30/60s per-IP rate-limit bucket on shared ingress IP. Same harness artefact documented in iteration_10 + iteration_11. Mitigation unchanged: ~80s cooldown between suites. Not a product defect — iter12 already passes 15/15 in isolation and all regressions pass cleanly with the cooldown.

ui_bugs.issue: Submit button is NOT client-side-disabled when 'Business justification' is <10 chars. The backend enforces the min-10 rule (returns 400), but UX lets the user click Submit with a 9-char reason and only finds out after the round-trip. Should disable Submit until len(trim(reason))>=10, or show inline error.

integration_issues.issue: When admin opens /access-review the 9 MFA-gated reports return 401 until re-auth. Frontend surfaces this with a toast 'Re-authentication required — please confirm your password on the Security page.' — correct default-deny behaviour, BUT the user is bounced to the Security page to reauth; the page does not offer an inline reauth prompt. Result: the 4 top-of-page stat cards (Active users / PHI reads 7d / Authz denials 7d / Break-glass 30d) remain empty on first load. Consider inline reauth modal on this page.

action_items: (LOW) Disable the Request-elevation dialog Submit button while reason <10 chars (currently validated only by backend).
(LOW) Offer inline reauth prompt on /access-review instead of redirecting the user to /security — today the stat cards sit empty behind a toast.
No product code changes needed for the passing tests; the two above are UX polish.
critical_code_review_comments: services/authz/constants.py cleanly separates roles, permissions, and the ROLE_GRANTS matrix; super_admin is correctly stripped of APR on governance actions (verified via /api/authz/matrix).

policy.require_permission default-deny is authoritative: patient→/authz/roles 403, doctor→/access/reports/privileged-users 403, and every denial writes action=authz.denied into the audit log (confirmed via GET /api/audit-logs?action=authz.denied).
MFA gate returns 401 + X-Reauth-Required:1 (not 403) — this is correct and lets the frontend differentiate 'please reauth' from 'you have no grant'.
Elevation lifecycle enforces separation-of-duties: admin POST /elevation/{own_id}/decision {approve} returns 400 with 'Separation' in detail. Good.
POST /authz/users/{uid}/roles response includes sessions_revoked=true and bumps session_epoch — old JWTs for that user are correctly invalidated immediately.
Dual-run shim /authz/me/permissions maps legacy admin→super_admin (51 perms), doctor→provider, staff→clinical_staff, patient→patient_portal (scope=self) — verified for all four seeded accounts.
Permission matrix page pulls from /api/authz/matrix (read-only evidence); no write path on the frontend — matches the 'backend is authoritative, frontend reflects' contract.
Zero CSP violations across /permissions /roles /access-review /elevation — CSP middleware from iter11 is unaffected by the new pages.

success_rate.backend: 100% — iter12: 15/15, iter7: 27/27, iter8+iter11: 32/32 (74/74 in isolation). Combined back-to-back run reproduces the same iter5/iter9 40-burst login-bucket collision already documented in iter10/iter11; product code is clean.
success_rate.frontend: ~95% — all required flows work; the two items in action_items above are UX polish, not blockers. 0 CSP violations. Sidebar + RBAC hiding correct for admin/doctor/patient.

test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md as-is. admin@ccms.app / Admin@ComplianceClinic1 for privileged calls; doctor/staff/patient identical to prior iterations. MFA not enrolled on seeded accounts — reauth path is password-only (POST /api/auth/reauth {password}).
seed_data_creation: Iteration 12 backend seed runs idempotently on startup: 11 roles / 115 perms / 11 grant groups — confirmed on live /api/authz/matrix. Tests only create ephemeral elevation-request rows (UUID-scoped) which remain in the elevation_requests collection; no PHI written.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 = RBAC + scopes + policy overlays + 4 admin pages (Roles, Permission matrix, Access review, Elevation). Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12_authz.py — 15/15 green against https ingress. Run ALL iteration tests with env: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://chiro-checkout-flow.preview.emergentagent.com CCMS_BASE_URL=https://chiro-checkout-flow.preview.emergentagent.com/api pytest …. IMPORTANT: tests hit localhost by default but require HTTPS because login cookies are Secure+SameSite=None — set CCMS_BASE_URL to the public URL or they all 401. Rate-limit cooldown rule from iter10/iter11 still applies: do not run iter5 or iter9's 40-burst login tests back-to-back with other suites without ~80s wait. The only 2 open items are LOW-priority frontend UX polish (see action_items). MOCKED: email/SMS comms (unchanged). HSTS suppressed by design (APP_ENV not production).
rca of the issue: No new bugs to RCA. Minor UX items: (1) Elevation dialog relies on backend for the 10-char validation — safer pattern is to mirror the constraint client-side (disable Submit button) so users see immediate feedback; (2) /access-review page uses a toast to signal 401 from the reports endpoints but does not offer an inline reauth prompt, so the 4 stat-card numbers stay empty on first load until the user visits /security and reauths. Both are cosmetic; authz enforcement itself (default-deny, MFA gate, separation-of-duties, audit) is correct.
